blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'd have done it again too.
The cafeteria had its own logic. Groups organized by weight — the heaviest social gravity pulled the biggest tables in the middle. Everyone else found their orbit from there.
Center table. Ten people already, and it was day one. Of course.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You found a table in half a day.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh they found me. There's a difference. Come on — open seat.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: That seat's two spots down from you.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh it is literally the same table. Stop analyzing and just sit.
Maya was near the windows. Already had a table. Keisha next to her. She looked up at exactly the right moment — the kind of timing that might be coincidence and might not be.
I'd been standing there for four seconds. In cafeteria time that's already a thing.

blocks.prompt: Four seconds. Everybody makes a decision in four seconds.

options.label: Jonathan's table. Walk through the room like you belong there.
Maya's table. She already knows your name.
Somewhere quiet. Don't make it a moment.
